seoTitle: Churchill Island vs Clarendon — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Churchill Island and Clarendon. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Churchill Island vs Clarendon: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Churchill Island and Clarendon.
heading: Churchill Island vs Clarendon — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Churchill Island, Victoria and Clarendon, Victoria. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Churchill Island has a lower overall climate risk score (35/100) compared to Clarendon (47/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Churchill Island or Clarendon safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Churchill Island has a flood risk score of 34/100, while Clarendon scores 56/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Churchill Island scores 18/100 for bushfire risk. Clarendon scores 64/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Churchill Island or Clarendon?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Churchill Island has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 2.55% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
